channel.title: Politics-Au on The Daily Thrum
channel.description: Recent content in Politics-Au on The Daily Thrum

item.title: Sydney Man Furious Cross Redhead Has Yet To Meet The Online Right During UK Trip
item.description:
SYDNEY — Local man Nathan Norton has expressed mounting frustration that Pauline Hanson’s current tour of Britain has included meetings with Holly Valance and Tommy Ten Names but, inexplicably, no confirmed encounter with Carl Benjamin.

Norton said the trip had initially shown enormous promise.

“She met Holly Valance. Good start,” he said.

“Then Tommy Robinson, Stephen Yaxley-Lennon, whatever we’re calling him this week. Fine. We’re building momentum.”

“But where the fuck are Sargon, Mr H and Dan Tubb?”

item.title: Albanese Apologises For Comment About Kylie Minogue, Then Clarifies He Had Not Meant To Suggest He “Wouldn't” If She Were Younger
item.description:
Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has apologised unequivocally for comments about Kylie Minogue before immediately clarifying that he had not meant to suggest he “wouldn’t” if she were younger, triggering a second and substantially more complicated round of apologies.

“I apologise unequivocally,” Albanese said. “The comment was inappropriate, disrespectful and not reflective of the standard Australians expect from their Prime Minister.”

“To be clear, she is almost 60. Not that she’s not pretty still. I don’t mean that. I mean, she may not generate the same level of interest as a younger—no, sorry, I’ve said that wrong.”

item.title: “Did You Stumble On Our Penis There, Brah?” China Tells Regional Governments After Missile Test
item.description:
China has dismissed Australian and Pacific island government concerns over its latest ballistic missile test, asking whether they had “stumbled on our penis there, brah?” after the weapon entered the South Pacific and briefly reminded regional governments whose is bigger matters, actually, and they can take their rules-based order and shove it up their diplomatic process.

Australian officials expressed concern over the launch, describing it as provocative, destabilising and insufficiently communicated to countries expected to remain calm while nuclear-capable delivery systems are demonstrated in their general direction.

item.title: Nation Finally Moves On From Multiculturalism Debate After Pauline Hanson Weighs In On Best Punisher
item.description:
Australia’s media class has finally moved on from debating whether the country is multicultural after Pauline Hanson triggered a fresh national controversy by arguing that Ray Stevenson was the best on-screen Punisher.

The remarks immediately derailed the programme after Hanson said “Thomas Jane was good, but Ray Stevenson really portrayed him as I imagined him when I was reading the comics”.

Within minutes, the country’s political and media infrastructure had pivoted to the more urgent question of who best portrayed the Marvel antihero.

item.title: Senator Warns Democracy Under Threat Unless Wrong Party Banned From Democratic Elections
item.description:
CANBERRA — Greens Senator Mehreen Faruqi has warned that Australian democracy is under threat from One Nation and that the only way to protect democracy may be to stop Australians voting for it.

Faruqi said One Nation should not be allowed to contest elections as a normal political party because it was, in her words, a “racist movement” seeking to rob Australia of its “multicultural vibrancy” and turn the country into a “bleak, bland society”.

item.title: Chinese Navy Floats Giant Aerial Drone Carrier Over Sydney
item.description:
SYDNEY — A giant People’s Liberation Army Navy aerial drone carrier floated over Sydney Harbour this morning before leaving unchallenged.

The platform, identified by Chinese state media as the Tiān Jī-class aerial drone carrier — roughly translated as Heavenly Strike — entered Sydney airspace at approximately 8:17am.

The enormous jet-propelled structure was roughly the size of a suburban shopping centre and was visibly equipped with drone bays, missile pods, sensor arrays and several glowing red lights.

item.title: Unvaccinated Cops Welcomed Back Years After Trauma Of The Century Memory-Holed
item.description:
SYDNEY — Police officers who refused the COVID vaccine have been warmly invited to return to the force years after being stood down, dismissed, shamed, isolated, treated as operational contaminants and informed that public service was impossible without total medical compliance.

The move comes after officials determined that enough time had passed for the entire episode to be discussed in the emotionally neutral language of “workforce recovery”, “re-engagement”, “lessons learned” and “moving forward”, rather than the more difficult language of “we bullied people during a panic and now need staff”.

item.title: Content Creator Host Focuses On Immigration-Housing Link After Realising “No Shelter” Harder To Lifestyle-Hack Than Traffic Or Medicare Collapse
item.description:
SYDNEY — A YouTube content creator has explained his continued focus on the relationship between immigration and housing, rather than immigration and every other overloaded national system, after realising that shelter remains one of the few social goods Australians cannot simply avoid needing through better habits, lower expectations, or leaving the house less.

Commentator Julian May, host of the podcast Mayday, uploaded a 52-minute video explaining why it won’t be long until the Royal Botanic Gardens becomes “an informal campground”, marking what viewers described as a logical progression for a man who has already spent several years warning that roads, hospitals, schools, energy grids and customer service departments were all approaching terminal failure.

item.title: Tony Abbott Raises Serious Concerns About Immigration Despite Tragically Never Having Had Any Political Influence
item.description:
SYDNEY — Former prime minister Tony Abbott has raised serious concerns about immigration, social cohesion and national identity, despite the tragic historical fact that he has never held any meaningful political power and therefore could not possibly have influenced any of the settings now troubling him.

Appearing in an interview, Mr Abbott cautioned that “people need to join Team Australia rather than simply live in Hotel Australia,” a phrase he delivered with the grave disappointment of a man who had apparently only recently discovered that slogans do not integrate people.

item.title: Albanese: “When We Get Elected We’ll Fix Everything”
item.description:
CANBERRA — Anthony Albanese has reminded voters that Labor remains fully committed to fixing everything once it gets elected and he becomes Prime Minister.

Speaking at a press conference about housing affordability, energy prices, crime, and the increasingly ambitious task of buying a lettuce without taking out a personal loan, Albanese said Australians should remain patient.

“We have a clear plan for government,” he said. “The important thing now is making sure we get the opportunity to implement it.”

item.title: Premier Who Doesn’t Know What A Woman Is Lambasts Billboard Truck, Saying “It Attacks Women”
item.description:
MELBOURNE — Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan, who has previously struggled to define what a woman is, has condemned a billboard truck portraying her as a witch, warning that the image represents an attack on women, a category which sources confirmed had briefly become legally, politically and rhetorically available again.

The billboard, which reportedly drove around Melbourne as part of a political campaign, prompted the Premier to denounce sexism in public life and warn that women in leadership should not be subjected to degrading language, imagery, or old-fashioned misogynistic tropes unless those women first require a departmental definition, stakeholder process, inclusive terminology review, and several footnotes.

item.title: Leaked Documents Reveal Victoria Initially Had Plans To Incinerate Unvaccinated Neighbourhoods With Superheated Plasma
item.description:
MELBOURNE — Leaked documents have revealed the Victorian government initially considered plans to incinerate unvaccinated neighbourhoods with superheated plasma during the pandemic, before officials reportedly decided the measure could be perceived as “slightly coercive” if announced before a press conference on community safety.

The draft proposal, titled Operation Final Dose: Targeted Thermal Confidence Measures For Persistent Non-Compliance Zones, outlined a phased strategy under which suburbs with insufficient vaccination uptake would receive escalating behavioural interventions, beginning with text-message reminders and ending with “high-temperature area normalisation”.

item.title: Grand Plan Announced To Accommodate Population Growth By Building Second Sydney And Melbourne Above First Sydney And Melbourne
item.description:
CANBERRA — The federal government has announced an ambitious long-term infrastructure plan to accommodate Australia’s growing population by building additional elevated street-level platforms above Sydney and Melbourne, creating a second CBD and surrounding suburbs directly on top of the existing ones by 2075.

The proposal, described as a “vertical urban continuity framework”, would see major roads, train stations, apartment towers, shopping centres, childcare centres, cafes, bike lanes, vape shops and unresolved social tensions duplicated approximately 300 metres above current street level.
